meta.title: Lift Plan — LOLER Reg 8 / BS 7121
meta.subtitle: Standard lift: 12.8 t AHU to level-6 plant deck, 50 t mobile crane
meta.author: Appointed Person
meta.classification: Lift plan — competent-person review required before lifting
meta.deid_notice: Input-derived persons are referred to by role label and no medical-fitness detail is carried. The appointed person, operator, and slinger are named as a deliberate competence record. The re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Classified STANDARD per BS 7121 (KB-DATA-LIFT-CATEGORIES): single crane, load a significant fraction of the SWL-at-radius, overhead-line and confined-radius proximity. A named appointed person and a documented lift plan are required. (A complex / tandem / blind lift, or a lift over an occupied area, would mandate an appointed-person written plan plus contingency / abort criteria.)

rows: 12.8 t (12.0 t load + 0.8 t rigging) — CONFIRMED
sections.caption: Load confirmed before planning — an unconfirmed weight is a [GAP] and a stop, never assumed.

sections.text: Equipment selection & SWL / utilisation (READ from the rated-capacity chart)

rows: 50 t mobile, 28 m boom, on outriggers
sections.caption: SWL-at-radius and utilisation are READ from the manufacturer's rated-capacity chart and checked against BS 7121 thresholds — NOT computed (no lifting calculator). Utilisation over the planned safe margin would flag re-selection of the crane.

items: Ground / outrigger bearing assessed against the outrigger loads and the site ground report; mats sized accordingly.
11 kV overhead line within the slew radius — see exclusion zones below.
Confined working radius adjacent to the occupied building elevation.

sections.text: Overhead-line control LEADS with elimination / engineered exclusion, NOT PPE: the lift is re-positioned so no part of the crane or load enters the GS6 minimum-clearance envelope of the 11 kV line; goal-posts and a barriered exclusion zone enforce the boundary, with a dedicated banksman. 'Operatives to take care / wear PPE' near the line is rejected as a PPE-only control.

rows: GS6 minimum clearance from the 11 kV line
Re-route + goal-posts + barriered exclusion + banksman (engineered exclusion, not PPE)
Barriered; no persons under a suspended load
Hard barrier + signage; lift timed outside occupancy where practicable
sections.caption: Exclusion zones set from the proximity tests (KB-DATA-LIFT-CATEGORIES).

items: 1. Rig the load to the 4 certified lifting points; check accessory certs and CoG.
2. Trial-lift / weigh: lift clear by 150 mm, confirm load stability and the chart weight.
3. Travel / slew within the planned arc only — banksman in continuous communication.
4. Place onto the level-6 plant deck at the planned set-down point.
5. De-rig and clear the exclusion zone.

sections.caption: Named as a deliberate competence record (legitimate). No medical-fitness / health detail is carried — fitness is recorded only as 'current medical certificate held' at role level.

items: ABORT on loss of communication between operator and slinger / signaller.
ABORT if in-service wind exceeds 9.8 m/s (chart limit).
ABORT on any unplanned obstruction or person entering an exclusion zone.
On abort: land the load at the nearest safe set-down, secure, and re-plan.

sections.text: Lift suspended if the in-service wind limit (9.8 m/s, from the rated-capacity chart) is forecast or measured at the boom tip, or in conditions that impair the slinger / signaller's visibility of the load.

rows.title: Overhead-line contact
rows.description: Initial High; reduced to Medium after re-route + engineered exclusion + banksman (risk_matrix residual re-score). No PPE-only treatment accepted.

rows.title: Load instability / dropped load
rows.description: Trial-lift / weigh confirms the chart weight and CoG before travel; certified accessories.

rows.control: Re-position the lift to eliminate entry into the GS6 overhead-line envelope
Engineered exclusion zone (goal-posts + barriers) at the line

rows.action: Confirm the ground / outrigger-loading report against the chart outrigger loads before set-up
Re-plan and re-brief on any change of crane, load, radius, ground, or weather beyond the limit

sections.text: Decision-support only. This lift plan must be reviewed by a competent person before any lifting operation proceeds; it does not constitute competent-person approval.

items: LOLER 1998 Reg 8 — lifting operations planned by a competent person, supervised, carried out safely
LOLER 1998 Reg 9 — thorough examination of the crane and lifting accessories
BS 7121 — Safe Use of Cranes: lift categorisation (basic / standard / complex)
ISO 45001 clause 6.1.2 / 8.1.2 — hazard ID & the hierarchy of controls
CDM 2015 — where the lift is part of construction works (the lift sits under the Construction Phase Plan)
